summary: The login/session root +0x70..+0x78 range is now mapped as a pending event queue. The queue holds 0x78-byte entries, each entry can own a 0x268-byte OTL request pointer at entry +0x70, and 0x14062B170 builds event telemetry requests for impression/click/dismissal entries before the drain helpers wait on and release outstanding request state.
confidence: high for queue start/end fields, 0x78 entry stride, entry +0x70 request pointer ownership, event selector literals, request allocation size, request submission, and request +0x224/+0x234 control fields; medium for semantic names of individual string-like path components.

root_field_map_delta.role: String-like root prefix copied to a stack string before event request formatting.
evidence: 0x14062B1BA reads [root+0x17] as the short/heap sentinel.
0x14062B1C9 reads [root+0x8] and [root] when the sentinel says the string is heap backed.
0x14002DBD0 copies the selected root string into the stack string at RSP+0x48.

root_field_map_delta.role: String-like path component passed as the second event-format substitution argument.
evidence: 0x14062B233 checks [root+0x2F].
0x14062B237 loads root+0x18 into R9 before formatting with %s/%s/%s/%s?userHash=%s.

root_field_map_delta.role: String-like userHash argument passed as the final event-format substitution argument.
evidence: 0x14062B1F6 checks [root+0x47].
0x14062B1FA loads root+0x30, with heap-pointer fallback at 0x14062B205.
0x14062B245 stores the selected value at stack argument slot +0x30 for the five-%s formatter, matching the userHash=%s tail of the format string.

root_field_map_delta.role: Active request gate used before marking pending-entry request objects.
evidence: 0x14062B05A compares [root+0x68] with zero before walking the pending-entry queue.
When root +0x68 is clear, the walker marks each live entry request at [entry_request+0x234] = 1.

root_field_map_delta.role: Pending event queue begin/end range. Entries are walked with a 0x78-byte stride and the end pointer is rewound to begin after release.
evidence: 0x14062ABF5 loads begin from [root+0x70] and 0x14062ABFE loads end from [root+0x78].
0x14062B0FA and 0x14062B0FE perform the same begin/end load pattern in the async drain helper.
The loops advance the entry cursor by 0x78 bytes.
0x14062AC55/0x14062AC51 and 0x14062B14F/0x14062B14B call 0x140172E60 with begin/end.
0x14062AC62, 0x14062B09A, and 0x14062B165 write [root+0x78] = [root+0x70] after releasing the range.

selector_values.evidence: 0x14062B19B compares the decremented selector with one and 0x14062B1A1 loads events/dismissal.

entry_layout.role: String-like event path component passed as the fourth substitution argument to %s/%s/%s/%s?userHash=%s.
evidence: 0x14062B208 reads [entry+0x1F] as the short/heap sentinel.
0x14062B20C loads entry+0x8 and 0x14062B217 switches to the heap pointer when required.
0x14062B24D stores the selected value into stack argument slot +0x28 before the formatter call.

entry_layout.role: Owned active event request pointer for this queue entry.
evidence: 0x14062B014 walks entries and calls 0x14062B170 only when [entry+0x70] is zero.
0x14062B28D computes entry+0x70, 0x14062B29B reads the old pointer, and 0x14062B29E stores the newly allocated request pointer there.
0x14062ABF9 and 0x14062B0B0 drain loops read [entry+0x70], inspect the referenced object's refcount at [request+0], and call 0x14062CA70 with 100 ms waits while the request remains externally live.
0x14062C8B0 receives root+0x70 as a vector/range pointer and scans entries by 0x78 stride while checking [entry+0x70].

related_global_fields.role: String-like path component passed as the third event-format substitution argument.
evidence: 0x14062B21A loads [0x140C4C9C8], 0x14062B221 adds 0x48, and 0x14062B225 checks the +0x17 string sentinel for that nested string.
0x14062B257 stores the selected value at stack argument slot +0x20 before the %s/%s/%s/%s?userHash=%s formatter call.

functions.role: Builds and submits one pending event request for an entry whose +0x70 request pointer is currently null.
behavior: Reads entry +0x00 to choose events/impression, events/click, or events/dismissal.
Copies a root string-like prefix into a stack string.
Formats %s/%s/%s/%s?userHash=%s using the event path, root +0x18, global 0x140C4C9C8 +0x48, entry +0x08, and root +0x30.
Allocates a 0x268-byte request object with 0x140793B40 and initializes it through 0x14062B7B0.
Swaps the new request pointer into entry +0x70 and cleans up/frees any previous pointer through 0x14062B8C0 and 0x1407B8478.
Writes request +0x224 = 5, then submits through 0x14062BCE0 with the formatted event path and literal data pointer 0x140B9AC18.

functions.role: Walks the pending queue and builds missing per-entry requests.
behavior: When the queue is finished, tail-jumps to 0x14062C8B0 with RCX = root +0x70 and EDX = 0.

functions.role: Marks live entry requests and releases the pending-entry range when the active root request gate is clear.
behavior: Requires root +0x68 to be zero before marking pending entries.
For each entry with a non-null request pointer, writes [request+0x234] = 1.
Calls 0x140172E60(root+0x70, root+0x78) and rewinds root +0x78 back to root +0x70.

functions.role: Drain loops used by close/flush and async teardown paths.
behavior: Read each entry +0x70 request pointer and inspect the referenced object's refcount at +0.
Call 0x14062CA70 with a stack-local 0x64 millisecond duration while the request remains externally live.
Release the queue range through 0x140172E60 and rewind the end pointer to begin.

functions.role: Vector/range helper reached with RCX = root +0x70. It scans the entry range and continues the same entry +0x70 request-state finalization pattern.

request_object_fields.role: Request submit/control value set to 5 before 0x14062BCE0.
Pending-entry mark/cancel flag set to 1 during queue cleanup.

literal_evidence.note: Literal data pointer passed as R8 to 0x14062BCE0 from 0x14062B2D5.

opennxt_947_server_implications: The client maintains a login/session event queue for impression, click, and dismissal telemetry. These requests are part of the same OTL request lifecycle as session open/close work, not unrelated UI-only state.
Server or harness traces should expect event endpoints shaped like events/<root-path>/<global-path>/<entry-path>?userHash=<root-userHash>, with exact path-component names still pending.
The client drains queued event requests during close/flush and teardown. Outstanding per-entry requests can delay cleanup in the same 100 ms wait chunks already mapped for the root wait helper.
A faithful OpenNXT 947 implementation should tolerate or emulate these telemetry endpoints if it wants the client bootstrap/login lifecycle to look natural under instrumentation.

Because request +0x234 is marked when the root active request slot is clear, server-side replay tooling should treat pending event cleanup and active session request cleanup as coordinated lifecycle phases.

reverser_tool_update.reason: Existing Reverser CLI capabilities were sufficient for this milestone: runtime-function windows, instruction windows, function literals, string reads, field refs, and callsite register tracing produced the required evidence without a cleanly scoped reusable code gap.
reverser_tool_update.next_native_capability_candidate: A vararg/stack-argument resolver for known format-call sites would make artifacts like 0x14062B170 easier to label automatically, especially when mapping %s substitutions to RCX/RDX/R8/R9 plus stack slots.

next_targets: Resolve semantic names for the root +0x18, global +0x48, entry +0x08, and root +0x30 path components.
Trace 0x14062BCE0 request submission internals so the request +0x224 value and literal 0x140B9AC18 role can be named.
Expand and summarize 0x14062C8B0..0x14062CA70 to finish the vector finalizer path.
Continue toward the close/flush indirect CALL R10 path in 0x14062AB70.
